product.name: BriefLink
product.purpose: Turn scattered company information into a shareable account brief for meetings, outreach, and handoffs.

summary.focus: Track coherence across the MVP loop from landing dashboard to intake, processing, and brief consumption/sharing.
summary.sourceOfTruth: Visible prototype and workspace artifacts for this cycle.
summary.replacementNote: This replaces the prior packaging-oriented dashboard with a lightweight product-coherence monitor.

items.goal: Orient the operator quickly and route them into brief creation or recent work.
checks: Primary action to start intake is visible
Recent or existing briefs can be surfaced
Empty and populated dashboard variants are defined

items.goal: Collect company context, links, notes, and source inputs needed to build a brief.
checks: Input collection state is distinct from processing state
Users can understand what data is being gathered
Partial completion and validation gaps are representable

items.goal: Communicate retrieval, synthesis, and brief generation progress without implying completion too early.
checks: Processing state is explicit and non-idle
Errors and stale results are distinguishable from in-progress work

items.goal: Show generated brief content clearly and support ready-to-share handoff behavior.
checks: Ready brief state is distinct from partial brief state
Share/export actions appear only when content is usable
Refresh or regeneration path is visible when content is stale

modules.name: Dashboard
modules.emptyDefinition: No briefs or no recent activity yet; emphasize primary CTA, explain value, and avoid dead-end empty chrome.
modules.populatedDefinition: Show recent briefs, current draft/progress, and next-most-likely action to continue work.

modules.name: Intake workspace
modules.emptyDefinition: No sources collected yet; present input affordances, examples, and minimum required fields.
modules.populatedDefinition: Display collected sources, notes, validation state, and ability to continue or submit for processing.

modules.name: Processing panel
modules.emptyDefinition: Not started; no progress UI should imply work is underway.
modules.populatedDefinition: Active progress or completed synthesis state with clear movement toward a generated brief.

modules.name: Brief viewer
modules.emptyDefinition: No brief generated yet; explain what will appear here after processing.
modules.populatedDefinition: Render structured brief sections, metadata, and sharing/export affordances.

states.name: empty
states.meaning: No user data, source material, or generated brief exists for the current surface.

states.name: collecting
states.meaning: User is adding or the system is gathering source inputs, but generation has not begun.

states.name: processing
states.meaning: Retrieval, synthesis, or generation is actively running.

states.name: ready
states.meaning: Content is complete enough for normal viewing and sharing.

states.name: partial
states.meaning: Some useful content exists, but expected sections or source coverage are incomplete.

states.name: error
states.meaning: A blocking failure prevented collection, processing, or retrieval.

states.name: stale/needs-refresh
states.meaning: Displayed content exists but is no longer trusted as current relative to new inputs or elapsed time.

guidance: Use card/list skeletons for recent briefs and activity rows.
Keep primary CTA visible even while supporting content loads.
Do not show fake metrics or decorative placeholders that imply nonexistent data.
Use field-level placeholders only where structure is known.
Represent pending source ingestion with item rows, not completed content blocks.
Keep validation and minimum-required-input messaging visible during collection.
Use a stable progress shell with section placeholders matching eventual brief layout.
Show that work is underway without inventing precise completion timing.
Preserve page structure so transition to ready state does not shift the interface unexpectedly.
Use section skeletons for summary, key facts, and narrative blocks while content resolves.
Hide or disable share/export until state is ready or explicitly permitted for partial output.
If stale, preserve current content but add visible refresh/regenerate treatment rather than blanking the page.

sections.title: Operational Contract Note
sections.body: For this cycle, inaccessible or omitted board artifact text is non-operative. Visible prototype files and visible workspace artifacts are operative evidence. Dashboard truth should reflect durable observable product artifacts, not missing proposal text or header-only board references.
